exemplar.title: MYP Personal Project: HOSA - Health Occupations Students of America club

detailedExemplarData.title: MYP Personal Project: HOSA - Health Occupations Students of America club

strengths: You effectively illustrate the ATL skill of Collaboration by detailing persistent communication with HOSA headquarters and student life offices.
Your adaptability and problem-solving are evident in how you sought teacher support to adjust your approach when challenges arose.
Including survey results (17 positive responses) serves as concrete evidence of applying collaboration and communication skills to gauge interest.
The development of member selection criteria and the structure of your application form showcase strong use of communication and self-management skills.
weaknesses: Your reflection on ATL use would be deepened by analyzing how communication strategies impacted member engagement metrics over time.
Consider explaining how specific ATL skills improved the quality of your final product, not just the process, to demonstrate impact.
Some examples are descriptive but would benefit from linking the skill to measurable outcomes or improvements observed.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: You have provided robust examples of applying ATL skills, particularly in collaboration and self-management, backed by survey data and procedural details. To strengthen this section, deepen your analysis of how these skills influenced engagement and product quality. Explicitly connect each ATL skill to measurable changesâsuch as response rates or improvements in meeting objectivesâto fully demonstrate their effectiveness.

strengths: Your learning goal is clearly articulated and powerfully linked to personal experiences, giving authentic motivation for reducing pill dependence.
You present multiple success criteria (Function, Time, Cost), showing a comprehensive understanding of the challenges and benchmarks for your project.
The intended productâestablishing HOSA in Costa Ricaâis ambitious yet well defined, demonstrating a clear vision and measurable outcomes.
Your Gantt-style plan offers precise sequencing and dates, indicating strong time management and feasibility planning.
weaknesses: Success criteria lack SMART specificityâmetrics such as target attendance rates or lesson completion goals should be defined to allow precise evaluation.
The Target Audience table is missing clear quantitative criteria; you should specify how many survey responses or members constitute success.
The Cost criterion needs a detailed budget breakdown and alternative funding strategies to strengthen feasibility and risk mitigation.
Your plan would benefit from explicit resource allocations (personnel, materials) and responsibility assignments for each task to clarify execution.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Overall, your planning demonstrates clear vision, authentic motivation, and detailed scheduling. You excel at defining why the project matters and when key milestones will occur. To elevate this work, refine each success criterion into SMART statements with quantitative targets, complete missing metrics in your audience analysis, and enhance your feasibility plan with budget details and resource assignments. This will ensure that your impressive vision is matched by robust, measurable planning.

strengths: Your evaluation of the Target Audience and Function criteria is thorough, using clear benchmarks to measure success.
You realistically appraise the Cost criterion and describe adaptive fundraising strategies, showing resilience and problem-solving.
You identify meaningful personal growth in leadership, communication, and adaptability, aligning well with your project goals.
Your reflection on recruiting challenges and lesson adaptation offers insightful evaluation of your learning process.
The closing reflection effectively summarizes successes and acknowledges collaboration with HOSA staff, providing a strong overall evaluation.
weaknesses: The Organization criterion evaluation is incomplete; you should specify next steps to meet your three-meeting target and outline corrective actions.
Your reflection would be more systematic if you evaluated each success criterion explicitly, rather than focusing on selected elements.
When discussing learner profile attributes, link them to specific examples of risk-taking and knowledge building to deepen your reflection.
The Time criterion analysis would benefit from a concise summary of lessons learned and concrete strategies for future planning.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflective work demonstrates strong self-awareness and honest evaluation of both successes and challenges. You effectively use evidence to assess multiple criteria and articulate personal growth. To enhance this reflection, include a structured review of each success criterion, outline actionable steps for partially met goals, and tie learner profile attributes to concrete instances in your process. A succinct lessons-learned summary for time management will also sharpen your insights for future projects.

annotations.comment: Consider reflecting on how this communication line impacted member engagement metrics to deepen your evaluation of ATL use.
Your evaluation of Target Audience and Function criteria clearly measures success against defined benchmarks, demonstrating thorough reflection.
Organization criterion is partially evaluated; specify next steps to meet the three-meeting target and embed corrective actions.
Your learning goal is clearly stated and linked to personal experience, providing strong motivation for the project.
Including multiple success criteria (Function, Time, Cost) shows comprehensive planning; you anticipate key challenges such as engagement, scheduling, and budget.
The learning goal is clear but could be made SMART by adding measurable success criteria (e.g., target attendance rates or lesson completion metrics).
Your success criteria section contains many considerations but lacks concise, measurable statements; refine each into SMART criteria with clear metrics.
Your personal reflection on mental health struggles powerfully establishes why you care about reducing pill dependence, giving authentic context for your learning goal.
The intended product of establishing HOSA in Costa Rica is well defined and ambitious, demonstrating a clear vision for impact.
Detailing the partial fulfillment of Cost criterion and adaptive fundraising strategies shows realistic evaluation and resilience.
The Target Audience table entry is incomplete in the âCriteriaâ cell and should fully articulate how many respondents signify success.
For the Cost criterion, include a detailed budget estimate and alternative funding strategies to strengthen feasibility.
You effectively identify development of leadership, communication, and adaptability, showcasing growth aligned with your project goals.
Your Gantt-style plan with specific goals and dates demonstrates clear sequencing and time management for achieving each stage.
Consider adding resource allocations (e.g., personnel, materials) or responsibilities in the notes to clarify how each task will be executed.
You effectively demonstrate the ATL of Collaboration by detailing persistent communication with HOSA headquarters and student life.
Your example of adapting your approach with teacher support shows reflection on problem-solving and effective use of ATL skills.
Survey results with 17 positive responses are strong evidence of applying collaboration skills to gauge student interest.
While identifying Learner Profile attributes is valuable, deepen your reflection by linking specific examples of risk-taking and knowledge-building.
Describing the member selection criteria and application form shows clear application of communication and self-management skills.
Your reflection on recruiting challenges and lesson adaptation offers insightful evaluation of your learning process.
Your reflection reaches a strong narrative but lacks explicit evaluation against each success criterionâconsider systematically reviewing criteria achievements.
The Time criterion evaluation is nuanced but would benefit from a concise summary and reflection on lessons learned for future planning.
Your general evaluation nicely summarizes successes and acknowledges effective collaboration with HOSA staff, providing a strong closing reflection.
